pullRequest.title: feat: add file-level summaries to index
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Closes #9

Adds per-file summaries to the code index, giving users a quick sense of what each file does before diving into individual symbols.

### Three-tier file summaries

1. **Module docstring extraction** (free) — extracts first sentence from file-level docstrings/comments for Python, JS/TS, Go, Rust, Java, PHP
2. **Heuristic from symbols** (free) — generates summaries like "Defines UserService class (3 methods)" or "Contains 5 functions: parse_file, ..."
3. **AI batch** (future) — placeholder for optional AI-powered summaries

### Changes

- **Data model** — `file_summaries: dict[str, str]` added to `CodeIndex`; `INDEX_VERSION` bumped 2→3 with backward compat (v2 indexes load fine via `.get()`)
- **`extract_module_docstring()`** — new function in `parser/extractor.py` supporting all 7 languages
- **`file_summarize.py`** — new module implementing the three-tier approach
- **`index_repo` / `index_folder`** — generate file summaries after parsing
- **`get_file_outline`** — includes `file_summary` in response
- **`get_file_tree`** — new `include_summaries` parameter (default false) to include summaries in tree
- **`server.py`** — updated schemas and call_tool passthrough

## Test plan

- [x] 17 new tests in `tests/test_file_summaries.py`
- [x] Storage round-trip and backward compat tests
- [x] All 185 tests pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

pullRequest.title: feat: expose incremental indexing via tool parameters
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Closes #10

The storage layer already has `detect_changes()` and `incremental_save()` implemented but no MCP tool exposes them. This PR adds an `incremental` boolean parameter to `index_repo` and `index_folder` tools.

### How it works

When `incremental=true` and an existing index exists:
1. Read current files and compute hashes
2. Call `detect_changes()` to identify changed/new/deleted files
3. Re-parse only changed + new files
4. Call `incremental_save()` instead of full `save_index()`

When no changes are detected, returns early with a "No changes detected" message.

### Changes

- **`index_folder.py`** — `incremental: bool = False` parameter with change detection + selective re-parse
- **`index_repo.py`** — same parameter for remote repos
- **`server.py`** — `incremental` added to both tool schemas and `call_tool()` passthrough

## Test plan

- [x] 5 new tests in `tests/test_incremental.py` (no changes, modified file, new file, deleted file, default full reindex)
- [x] All 173 tests pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

pullRequest.title: feat: add watch CLI subcommand for automatic incremental re-indexing
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Closes #112

- Adds a `watch` CLI subcommand that monitors one or more directories for filesystem changes and triggers incremental re-indexing automatically
- Uses `watchfiles` (Rust-based, async) for efficient OS-native filesystem notifications with configurable debounce
- Restructures `main()` to use argparse subcommands (`serve`, `watch`) while maintaining full backwards compatibility — bare `jcodemunch-mcp` and legacy flags like `--transport` continue to work
- Adds `watchfiles>=1.0.0` as an optional dependency under `[watch]` extras

### Usage

```bash
# Install with watch support
pip install jcodemunch-mcp[watch]

# Watch a single repo
jcodemunch-mcp watch /path/to/monorepo

# Watch multiple dirs (e.g., repo + worktrees)
jcodemunch-mcp watch /path/to/repo ~/.claude-worktrees/repo/worktree1

# Custom debounce, no AI summaries
jcodemunch-mcp watch --debounce 5000 --no-ai-summaries /path/to/repo
```

## Test plan

- [x] All 669 existing tests pass
- [x] `jcodemunch-mcp --help` shows both `serve` and `watch` subcommands
- [x] `jcodemunch-mcp watch --help` shows correct options
- [x] Backwards compat: `jcodemunch-mcp --transport stdio` still works
- [x] Smoke tested: watcher detects file touch, triggers incremental re-index, shuts down cleanly on signal

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

pullRequest.title: feat: auto-discover and watch Claude Code worktrees
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- Adds `watch-claude` CLI subcommand that auto-discovers and watches Claude Code worktrees via hook-driven events (JSONL manifest at `~/.claude/jcodemunch-worktrees.jsonl`) and/or `git worktree list` polling (`--repos` mode)
- Adds `hook-event` CLI subcommand (`jcodemunch-mcp hook-event create|remove`) for use in Claude Code's `WorktreeCreate`/`WorktreeRemove` hooks
- Both discovery modes can run concurrently with deduplication; worktree removal triggers watcher cleanup and index invalidation

Closes #123

## Changes

- **`src/jcodemunch_mcp/hook_event.py`** (new) — reads hook JSON from stdin, appends to JSONL manifest
- **`src/jcodemunch_mcp/watcher.py`** — adds `watch_claude_worktrees()`, manifest watcher, git poller, and per-worktree watcher lifecycle management
- **`src/jcodemunch_mcp/server.py`** — registers `hook-event` and `watch-claude` subcommands in CLI parser
- **`tests/test_watch_claude.py`** (new) — unit tests for hook event handling, manifest parsing, git worktree discovery, and watcher lifecycle
- **Docs** — QUICKSTART (detailed setup for both modes), ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, README, SPEC all updated

## Test plan

- [ ] `jcodemunch-mcp hook-event create` with piped JSON writes correct JSONL
- [ ] `jcodemunch-mcp hook-event remove` with piped JSON writes correct JSONL
- [ ] `jcodemunch-mcp watch-claude` with manifest watches and reacts to new entries
- [ ] `jcodemunch-mcp watch-claude --repos /path/to/repo` discovers existing worktrees
- [ ] Worktree removal triggers watcher stop and index cleanup
- [ ] Both modes running together do not double-watch
- [ ] Works on macOS, Linux, and Windows

pullRequest.title: feat: SQLite WAL storage backend with JSON migration
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Replace monolithic JSON index files with SQLite WAL databases. Zero new dependencies (`sqlite3` is Python stdlib).

- **99% reduction in disk writes** — delta rows (1-50 KB) instead of full JSON rewrites (1.5-33 MB per change)
- **90% faster cold reads** — SQLite page reads (~5-20ms) vs full JSON parse (50-200ms)
- **10x faster index updates** — debounce drops from 2000ms to 200ms (writes are now cheap)
- **Near-instant multi-process consistency** — WAL provides read consistency across concurrent MCP processes, no mtime polling or stale caches
- **Better crash safety** — ACID transactions + WAL replay vs atomic file rename
- **Zero new dependencies** — `sqlite3` ships with Python
- **Backward compatible** — auto-migrates existing JSON indexes, keeps `.json.migrated` for rollback

Resolves #136.

## Problem

Every file change triggers a full rewrite of the entire index JSON file (1.5-33 MB) via atomic temp-file + rename. With AI agents as the primary consumers of jcodemunch-mcp, writes are far more frequent than human development:

| Scenario | Index Size | Writes/hour | Disk I/O/hour |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Human developer, small repo | 1.5 MB | ~20 | 30 MB |
| Human developer, large repo | 33 MB | ~20 | 660 MB |
| AI agent, small repo | 1.5 MB | ~720 | 1,080 MB |
| AI agent, large repo | 33 MB | ~720 | 23,760 MB |

Additionally:
- **Multi-process stale reads**: Multiple MCP server processes share `~/.code-index/`. Each uses an LRU cache keyed on file mtime. Other processes don't see watcher updates until their next mtime check triggers a full JSON re-parse (50-200ms for large indexes).
- **Debounce overhead**: The 2-second debounce exists because JSON rewrites are expensive, creating a 2-second stale window after every file change.

## Implementation

### SQLite WAL backend (`sqlite_store.py` — new, 1,059 lines)

- **Schema**: 3 tables (`meta`, `symbols`, `files`) with indexed columns for fast lookup
- **WAL mode**: Set on every connection via `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL` — enables concurrent readers during writes
- **Connection pooling**: `busy_timeout=5000ms` prevents lock contention under concurrent access
- **Batch operations**: `executemany()` for all bulk inserts (symbols, files) — single transaction per save
- **Incremental save**: Only `INSERT OR REPLACE` rows for changed/new files + `DELETE` for removed files — unchanged data stays untouched
- **Mtime fast-path**: `detect_changes_with_mtimes()` stores `mtime_ns INTEGER` (nanosecond precision from `os.stat().st_mtime_ns`). Files whose mtime matches the stored value skip hashing entirely. Files where mtime changed but hash is identical ("touch-only") get their new mtime persisted so the *next* cycle is a cache hit.
- **Checkpoint-on-close**: `checkpoint_and_close()` runs `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` to compact the WAL file on server shutdown

### IndexStore delegation (`index_store.py` — modified)

`IndexStore` now delegates all storage operations to `SQLiteIndexStore` while maintaining backward compatibility:

- **`save_index`**: Delegates to SQLite, then cleans up any legacy `.json` file (renames to `.json.migrated`)
- **`load_index`**: Tries SQLite first, falls back to auto-migration from JSON if `.db` doesn't exist yet
- **`list_repos`**: Two-pass listing (SQLite first, then legacy JSON) with **eager migration** — any JSON-only repos discovered during listing are migrated to SQLite on the spot, closing the race window where `invalidate_cache` could destroy unmigrated data
- **`delete_index`**: Only deletes legacy `.json` files if a `.db` already existed (data was safely migrated). If the JSON is the sole copy, it is preserved to prevent silent data loss
- **`incremental_save`**: Delegates to SQLite, includes mtime-stickiness fix — files where mtime changed but content didn't get their new mtime persisted in the same transaction

### JSON to SQLite migration (`migrate_from_json`)

- Reads the full JSON index, writes all data into a new `.db` file within a single transaction
- Renames the original `.json` to `.json.migrated` (user can rollback by renaming back)
- Cleans up sidecars (`.meta.json`, `.json.sha256`, `.json.lock`)
- Triggered lazily on `load_index` and eagerly on `list_repos`

### Watcher debounce (`watcher.py`)

- `DEFAULT_DEBOUNCE_MS` reduced from 2000ms to 200ms — SQLite delta writes are cheap enough to run 10x more frequently

## New files

| File | Lines | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `src/jcodemunch_mcp/storage/sqlite_store.py` | 1,059 | Full SQLite backend — schema, CRUD, migration, checkpoint |
| `tests/test_sqlite_store.py` | 313 | SQLite-specific unit tests |
| `tests/test_local_integration.py` | 490 | End-to-end WAL behavior + migration safety tests |
| `tests/test_migration.py` | 72 | JSON to SQLite migration round-trip |

## Modified files

| File | Change |
| --- | --- |
| `src/jcodemunch_mcp/storage/index_store.py` | Delegate to SQLiteIndexStore, eager migration in `list_repos`, safe `delete_index`, legacy JSON cleanup in `save_index` |
| `src/jcodemunch_mcp/server.py` | Minor wiring for new storage backend |
| `src/jcodemunch_mcp/tools/index_folder.py` | File discovery performance optimization (see Bonus section) |
| `src/jcodemunch_mcp/watcher.py` | Debounce 2000ms to 200ms |
| `tests/test_find_importers.py` | Updated for SQLite storage |
| `tests/test_hardening.py` | Updated for SQLite storage |
| `tests/test_mtime_optimization.py` | Updated for SQLite storage |
| `tests/test_retrieval_tools.py` | Updated for SQLite storage |
| `tests/test_storage.py` | Updated for SQLite storage |

## Test plan

- [x] Full test suite: 823 passed, 4 skipped (Windows symlink edge cases)
- [x] End-to-end indexing verified on real repos (91-file and 358-file projects)
- [x] WAL file presence: `.db-wal` and `.db-shm` exist while connections are open
- [x] WAL mode pragma: every connection returns `journal_mode = wal`
- [x] Incremental save writes only delta rows (WAL growth < 50% of full DB size)
- [x] Mtime fast-path stickiness: cycle 2 after a touch skips re-hashing entirely
- [x] Multi-process consistency: second `IndexStore` instance sees first's commits immediately
- [x] Concurrent reader/writer: threaded read/write raises no errors
- [x] Checkpoint compacts WAL to 0 bytes on close
- [x] Migration safety: `delete_index` preserves unmigrated `.json` files
- [x] `list_repos` eagerly migrates JSON to SQLite (prevents race with `invalidate_cache`)
- [x] `save_index` cleans up zombie `.json` files after successful SQLite write
- [x] Migration round-trip: all fields preserved (symbols, hashes, mtimes, imports, context_metadata)

## Bonus: file discovery optimization (unrelated to SQLite)

Profiling a full index run revealed that **76% of total time was spent in file discovery**, not parsing or storage. The bottleneck was redundant pathlib operations in the inner loop:

- `Path.resolve()` called 2-3x per file (once in `validate_path`, once for `relative_to`, once for `_is_gitignored`)
- `validate_path()` re-resolved the already-resolved root directory on every iteration
- `Path.relative_to()` called with try/except for every file against every gitignore spec

Optimized in the last commit by resolving each file once, inlining the traversal check, and replacing `Path.relative_to()` with string prefix matching:

| Metric | Before | After | Change |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Full index (358 files, 5,666 symbols) | 18.9s | 9.0s | **2.1x faster** |
| File discovery phase | 14.2s | 3.8s | **3.8x faster** |
| `Path.resolve()` calls | 51,701 | 18,193 | -65% |
| `Path.relative_to()` calls | 100,836 | 0 | -100% |
| Total function calls | 32.1M | 5.9M | -82% |

No functional changes — identical file counts, symbol counts, and gitignore filtering verified across multiple repos.

Fixes issue #136

pullRequest.title: feat: batch array params + check_references — reduce tool call overhead by up to 10x
pullRequest.body:
---

## Summary

Fixes Issue #144 

Reduces LLM tool call round-trips for multi-file queries by adding batch array parameters to existing tools and a new composite tool for dead-code detection.

- `find_importers`: new `file_paths` array param (query N files in 1 call)
- `find_references`: new `identifiers` array param (query N identifiers in 1 call)
- `get_file_outline`: new `file_paths` array param (query N files in 1 call)
- `check_references`: new composite tool combining import + content search in 1 call
- Response-level `tip` fields guide models toward batch usage without external config

## Motivation

Benchmark ([#130](https://github.com/jgravelle/jcodemunch-mcp/issues/130)) comparing jCodeMunch vs native Grep/Glob/Read on dead-code detection found:

| Metric | Native (A) | jCodeMunch (B) | Ratio |
|--------|-----------|----------------|-------|
| Mean tool calls | 26.6 | 38.8 | 1.46x |
| Mean cost/iteration | $0.059 | $0.125 | 2.11x |
| Cache read tokens | 39,164 | 94,980 | 2.43x |

The 46% extra tool calls came from the `get_file_outline` + `find_importers` two-step pattern repeated per file. Each extra call compounds cache reads (full conversation history re-sent every turn).

## Design choices

**Array params on existing tools (not a generic batch tool):**
A generic `batch` meta-tool was considered and rejected. LLMs don't discover meta-tools well without explicit CLAUDE.md guidance. Array params on tools the model already uses have zero discovery cost. Response-level `tip` fields make them self-teaching — the model sees "Tip: use file_paths=[...] to query multiple files in one call" on the first call and switches for subsequent calls.

**`check_references` as a composite tool (not array params):**
Dead-code detection needs two *different* tools combined (import-level + content-level search). This can't be solved with array params on a single tool. `check_references` returns a single `is_referenced: bool` — the exact answer the model needs.

**No ripgrep/grep dependency:**
`search_text` already does cross-platform regex over cached UTF-8 content. Adding ripgrep would introduce a platform-dependent external dependency for marginal speed gains when the real bottleneck is LLM round-trips.

## Expected impact

For the dead-code benchmark pattern (`get_file_outline` per file + `find_importers` per file):

| Metric | Before | After (projected) |
|--------|--------|-------------------|
| Tool calls for 10 files | 20 | 2 (batch outline + batch importers) |
| Cache re-reads per turn | 20x schema payload | 2x schema payload |

For "is this symbol used?":

| Metric | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Tool calls | 2 (`find_references` + `search_text`) | 1 (`check_references`) |

## Schema changes (non-breaking)

| Tool | Before | After |
|------|--------|-------|
| `find_importers` | `required: ["repo", "file_path"]` | `required: ["repo"]`, new `file_paths` array param |
| `find_references` | `required: ["repo", "identifier"]` | `required: ["repo"]`, new `identifiers` array param |
| `get_file_outline` | `required: ["repo", "file_path"]` | `required: ["repo"]`, new `file_paths` array param |
| `check_references` | *new tool* | `required: ["repo"]`, `identifier` or `identifiers` |

Existing callers passing singular params get identical response shapes. Mutual exclusivity (singular vs array) enforced at function level via `ValueError`, caught by server's generic exception handler.

## Implementation details

- Each modified tool extracted into `_single` / `_batch` helpers with shared index loading
- `find_importers` batch: two-pass approach builds `files_that_are_imported` set first, then `import_map` — ensures `has_importers` annotation correctness
- `find_references` batch: reverse `ident_map` with per-file dedup, first-match-wins (named > specifier_stem), only indexes stems matching queried identifiers
- `check_references`: skips defining files in content search (finding symbol in its own file is not a reference), limits by files not lines
- Batch `check_references` strips per-entry `repo`/`_meta` for consistency with other batch tools
- `get_file_outline` batch delegates to `_single` per file (pragmatic — symbol tree building is complex), strips per-entry tips

## Commits

1. `feat: add file_paths array param to find_importers for batch queries`
2. `feat: add identifiers array param to find_references for batch queries`
3. `feat: add file_paths array param to get_file_outline for batch queries`
4. `feat: add check_references composite tool for dead-code detection`
5. `feat: add response-level tips guiding models toward batch params`
6. `fix: O(N*M) batch perf, content_count per-file, timing accuracy, and add missing tests`
7. `fix: has_importers race, batch dedup, redundant lowercasing, and missing edge-case tests`

## Test plan

- [x] `find_importers` batch: multiple files, empty list, singular backward compat, both-params error
- [x] `find_references` batch: multiple identifiers, empty list, singular backward compat, both-params error
- [x] `get_file_outline` batch: multiple files, empty list, both-params error
- [x] `check_references`: import ref, content ref, no refs, search_content=false, batch, unindexed repo, old index (no import data), neither-params error, both-params error
- [x] Server: tool count updated to 25, `check_references` in tool list
- [x] Full suite: **845 passed**, 4 skipped, 0 failures

Resolves #144

pullRequest.title: feat: watcher backpressure, index freshness signaling, and deferred summarization
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Adds per-repo backpressure signaling so AI agents know when the index is stale, reduces watcher reindex latency through a memory hash cache and deferred summarization, and introduces a `wait_for_fresh` MCP tool plus a `--freshness-mode=strict` server option for correctness-critical multi-agent deployments.

Closes #153 

## Why

In multi-agent scenarios, agents write files and immediately query the index for updated symbols. The watcher debounce is 200ms but a single reindex takes ~217ms for real edits (150KB file). Agents consistently read stale data with **no way to detect it** — every response looks the same whether the index is 0ms or 500ms behind.

Two factors make this unpredictable:
- **File sizes vary** — larger files push reindex time past the debounce window
- **Host CPU load varies** — other processes steal cycles, causing sporadic slowdowns

Without backpressure, agents make decisions based on outdated code. This is especially dangerous for rename/refactor workflows where reading stale symbols can cascade into broken edits.

## What changed

### New: `reindex_state.py` — per-repo state tracking

Each watched repo gets independent reindex state with `threading.Event`-based signaling:

| Function | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `mark_reindex_start(repo)` | Sets reindexing flag, clears event, records `stale_since` |
| `mark_reindex_done(repo)` | Clears reindexing, sets event, resets `stale_since` and failure counter |
| `mark_reindex_failed(repo, error)` | Sets event (unblocks waiters), increments failure counter, preserves `stale_since` |
| `get_reindex_status(repo)` | Returns `_meta`-ready dict: `index_stale`, `reindex_in_progress`, `stale_since_ms` |
| `wait_for_fresh_result(repo, timeout_ms)` | Blocks on event until reindex completes or timeout |
| `await_freshness_if_strict(repo, timeout_ms)` | Strict-mode auto-wait for read query tools |

Failure escalation: 1st failure → only `index_stale: true` (transient tolerance). 2nd+ → adds `reindex_error` and `reindex_failures` to `_meta`.

### New: `_meta` staleness fields on all query responses

```json
{
  "_meta": {
    "index_stale": true,
    "reindex_in_progress": true,
    "stale_since_ms": 47,
    "reindex_error": "parse failed",
    "reindex_failures": 2
  }
}
```

### New: `wait_for_fresh` MCP tool

Agents call this after writing files to block until the index catches up:

```json
{"fresh": true, "waited_ms": 0}
{"fresh": true, "waited_ms": 123}
{"fresh": false, "waited_ms": 500, "reason": "timeout"}
{"fresh": false, "waited_ms": 0, "reason": "reindex_failed", "reindex_error": "...", "reindex_failures": 3}
```

### New: `--freshness-mode=strict` CLI flag

Server-enforced freshness — all read query tools automatically wait up to 500ms for in-progress reindexes. Write tools (`index_folder`, `index_repo`, `index_file`, `invalidate_cache`) and utility tools (`list_repos`, `get_session_stats`, `wait_for_fresh`) are excluded.

Priority: `--freshness-mode=strict` > `JCODEMUNCH_FRESHNESS_MODE=strict` > `relaxed` (default)

### Speed: watcher memory hash cache

The watcher maintains an in-memory `dict[str, str]` mapping `rel_path → content_hash`. On each debounce tick, it compares incoming hashes against memory instead of loading the full SQLite index (~57ms savings). Changed files are communicated as `WatcherChange(change_type, abs_path, old_hash)` NamedTuples.

### Speed: deferred summarization

The fast path splits indexing into two phases:
1. **Immediate** (critical path): tree-sitter parse → `incremental_save` with empty summaries
2. **Deferred** (background daemon thread): AI summarization → second `incremental_save`

A monotonic generation counter prevents stale deferred work from overwriting fresher data.

### Docs: SPEC.md update

- 7 previously undocumented tools: `find_importers`, `find_references`, `check_references`, `search_columns`, `get_dependency_graph`, `get_session_stats`, `wait_for_fresh`
- 8 new environment variables
- New sections: Watcher and Index Freshness, Transport Modes and CLI
- Updated: Response Envelope, Indexing Semantics, Watch mode, Environment Variables

## Test results

### Unit tests

```
939 passed, 4 skipped, 0 failures
```

New test files (49 tests added):
- `tests/test_reindex_state.py` — 27 tests: state lifecycle, failure escalation, stale_since, generation counter, strict mode
- `tests/test_wait_for_fresh.py` — 12 tests: tool registration, `_meta` injection, strict mode timing, CLI flag parsing
- `tests/test_watcher_memory_cache.py` — 7 tests: WatcherChange compat, `_build_hash_cache` integration, fast-path deletion
- `tests/test_deferred_summarization.py` — 3 tests: `parse_immediate` returns without AI, timing

### Dynamic profiling (72 runtime checks)

Exercises real runtime paths — not mocked:

```
1. Reindex State Lifecycle          — 17/17 passed
2. wait_for_fresh (real threads)    — 11/11 passed
3. index_folder Fast Path           — 10/10 passed
4. _build_hash_cache Integration    —  6/6  passed
5. _meta Staleness Injection        — 13/13 passed
6. Strict Freshness Mode            —  2/2  passed
7. Deferred Summarization Split     —  4/4  passed
8. WatcherChange Compatibility      — 11/11 passed (all access patterns: property, index, unpack, isinstance)
```

### Performance measurements

| Path | Latency | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| Full index (2 files, 4 symbols) | 94ms | Baseline |
| Fast path (no hash cache) | 50ms | Skips directory discovery |
| Fast path (with hash cache) | 43ms | Also skips SQLite load |
| Fast path deletion | 46ms | File correctly removed from index |
| `parse_immediate` (3 symbols) | 4ms | No AI summarization blocking |
| `wait_for_fresh` (already fresh) | <1ms | Returns immediately |
| `wait_for_fresh` (50ms reindex) | 51ms | Blocks until event is set |
| Strict mode wait | 52ms | `asyncio.to_thread` dispatch overhead <2ms |

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests: 939 passed, 0 failures
- [x] Dynamic profiling: 72/72 runtime checks passed
- [x] Reindex state lifecycle: idle → start → done, idle → start → fail (×2) → done
- [x] `wait_for_fresh`: unknown repo, already-done, wait-with-signal, timeout, persistent failure
- [x] Fast path: with/without hash cache, deletion, mtime-only (no content change)
- [x] `_meta` injection: idle state, reindexing state, fresh-after-done, non-repo tools
- [x] Strict mode: real `asyncio.to_thread` dispatch with background thread completion
- [x] `_build_hash_cache`: split repo_id works, old unsplit path correctly raises `ValueError`
- [x] WatcherChange: property access, index access, unpacking, isinstance, default old_hash
- [x] Deferred summarization: `parse_immediate` returns without AI summaries in <5ms

pullRequest.title: feat: cross-platform path mapping via JCODEMUNCH_PATH_MAP
pullRequest.body:
Closes #157

## Summary

This is a feature request (and accompanying implementation) to add cross-platform
path remapping support via a new `JCODEMUNCH_PATH_MAP` environment variable —
enabling indexes built on one OS to be reused on another when the same files live
at different root paths.

## Motivation

Users who sync projects across machines (Nextcloud, Dropbox, network drives) have
their codebase rooted at different paths depending on OS:

- Linux: `/home/user/Nextcloud/Dev`
- Windows: `C:\Nextcloud\Dev`

Previously, indexes were not portable across machines because absolute paths are
baked in at index time. Every path stored in the index points to the build-time
root. When an agent on the other OS tried to look up the repo or retrieve file
content, those paths resolved to nothing — even though the files were physically
present on disk.

The only workaround was to re-index on every machine, which eliminates the value
of syncing the index alongside the source.

## What was implemented

- **New module** (`src/jcodemunch_mcp/path_map.py`): Exports `ENV_VAR =
  "JCODEMUNCH_PATH_MAP"` and two functions. `parse_path_map()` reads the env var,
  splits on `,` then splits each pair on the **last** `=` (`rsplit("=", 1)`) so
  `=` signs in path components are preserved, strips whitespace, logs a WARNING
  and skips malformed entries, returns `[]` when the var is unset or empty.
  `remap(path, pairs, reverse=False)` normalises both path and prefix to forward
  slashes for comparison, applies the first matching prefix substitution, and
  outputs using `os.sep` so results are native on the target platform.
  `reverse=True` swaps the substitution direction (current-machine path → stored
  path, used for hash lookups). Prefix matching is strict and directory-boundary
  aware: `/home/user` matches `/home/user/project` but not `/home/username`.
  First match wins; put more-specific prefixes before broader ones when they
  overlap.

- **Repo hash lookup — `index_folder`** (`tools/index_folder.py` lines 448, 701):
  At both `_local_repo_name` call sites (watcher fast path and standard discovery
  path), the caller-supplied `folder_path` is reverse-remapped before hashing so
  the resulting repo name matches the one stored in the index. `folder_path` is
  unchanged for the actual file walk — only the hash derivation uses the remapped
  value.

- **Repo hash lookup — watcher** (`watcher.py` lines 274, 751): Same treatment
  for `_local_repo_id` (the watcher's independent reimplementation of the same
  hash logic) at the `_watch_single` startup and `_stop_watching` cleanup sites.

- **`list_repos` display — SQLite backend** (`storage/sqlite_store.py` line 854):
  `source_root` is forward-remapped in `_list_repo_from_db` before being returned
  to callers, so agents on the current machine see paths that are meaningful to
  them.

- **`list_repos` display — legacy JSON backend** (`storage/index_store.py` line
  645): Same forward remap applied in `_repo_entry_from_data`, in the `else`
  branch only — the existing `JCODEMUNCH_REDACT_SOURCE_ROOT` path is untouched.

- **Config display** (`server.py`, `_run_config`): `JCODEMUNCH_PATH_MAP` row
  added to the Core section, using `ENV_VAR` imported from `path_map.py` to avoid
  string duplication. Displays the active value or `(none)` when unset.

- **Test suite** (`tests/test_path_map.py`, 21 tests): unit tests for
  `parse_path_map` and `remap` (including edge cases: `=` in path, malformed
  entries, mixed separators, directory-boundary matching); integration tests for
  the SQLite and legacy JSON backends, incremental re-use with a remapped path,
  and a watcher smoke test.

## Notes

- **Backwards compatible** — `JCODEMUNCH_PATH_MAP` is opt-in. When unset,
  `parse_path_map()` returns `[]` and `remap()` returns every path unchanged.
  Existing indexes, deployments, and workflows are completely unaffected.
- **Performance** — `parse_path_map()` is a lightweight `os.environ.get` + string
  split; stateless by design. Touch points in `index_folder.py` and `watcher.py`
  cache the result in a local `_pairs` variable when calling `remap()` more than
  once per operation.
- **Security / safety** — remapping only applies to paths already present in
  stored index metadata. No new filesystem access is introduced beyond existing
  behaviour.
- Follows the same `source_root`-transformation pattern as
  `JCODEMUNCH_REDACT_SOURCE_ROOT`.

## Test plan

- [x] All 21 new tests pass (`pytest tests/test_path_map.py`)
- [x] Full suite passes (940 tests, 0 failures introduced)
- [x] `list_repos` returns remapped `source_root` when `JCODEMUNCH_PATH_MAP` is set
- [x] `index_folder` with remapped path finds existing index without re-indexing
- [x] Unset `JCODEMUNCH_PATH_MAP` leaves all existing behaviour unchanged
- [x] Directory-boundary matching: `/home/user` does not match `/home/username`
- [x] Mixed separators: `C:/Users` matches stored `C:\Users` and vice versa
- [x] `jcodemunch-mcp config` displays the active mapping value

pullRequest.title: feat: add resolve_repo tool for O(1) path-based repo lookup
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- Adds `resolve_repo` MCP tool that resolves a filesystem path to its indexed repo identifier in O(1) — no scanning of all repos needed
- Computes the deterministic repo ID from path hash (same formula as `_local_repo_id` / `_local_repo_name`) and checks index existence directly
- Accepts repo roots, worktrees, subdirectories, and file paths; walks up to git root for subdirectory lookups

Closes #159

## Motivation

With 15+ local repos indexed (plus worktrees), `list_repos` returns ~36k tokens just to find a single repo entry. Every new conversation pays this cost. `resolve_repo` replaces that with a ~200 token targeted lookup.

## Changes

- **`src/jcodemunch_mcp/tools/resolve_repo.py`** (new) — core tool implementation
- **`src/jcodemunch_mcp/server.py`** — import, tool schema, dispatch
- **`tests/test_resolve_repo.py`** (new) — 8 test cases
- **`tests/test_server.py`** — updated tool count 24→25

## Test plan

- [x] All 8 new tests pass (`pytest tests/test_resolve_repo.py`)
- [x] Full suite passes (790 tests, 0 failures)
- [x] Exact indexed root → returns metadata
- [x] Subdirectory → resolves via git root
- [x] Non-indexed path → returns computed ID with hint
- [x] Nonexistent path → returns error
- [x] File path → uses parent directory
- [x] ID matches `_local_repo_id` for same path

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

pullRequest.title: feat(config): centralized JSONC configuration with language filtering, tool gating, and meta control
pullRequest.body:
## Why

jcodemunch-mcp has grown to **24+ environment variables**. This creates three problems:

1. **No per-project customization.** A Python-only monorepo still loads all 25+ language parsers, the dbt context provider, and the SQL preprocessor on every indexing run. There is no way to say "this project only uses Python."

2. **No tool-level control.** Every client gets every tool and every `_meta` field. The `suppress_meta` parameter was duplicated across all 27 tool schemas, adding **~594 tokens per `list_tools` call** just for a boolean that most clients never use.

3. **Env vars don't compose.** Setting `JCODEMUNCH_MAX_FOLDER_FILES` in a shell profile applies globally. If one project needs 5,000 files and another needs 500, you juggle env vars per terminal session.

## What

Fixes #161 
A centralized `.jsonc` config file that replaces scattered env vars with structured, per-project configuration:

```
~/.code-index/config.jsonc          # global defaults
<project-root>/.jcodemunch.jsonc    # per-project overrides (version-controlled)
```

### New capabilities (not possible with env vars alone)

| Feature | Config key | Effect |
|---------|-----------|--------|
| Language filtering | `languages` | Skip tree-sitter parsing for unconfigured languages |
| Tool disabling | `disabled_tools` | Remove tools from schema and block execution |
| Meta field control | `meta_fields` | `null` = all, `[]` = strip `_meta`, list = named fields only |
| Description overrides | `descriptions` | Flat string or nested dict format for tool + param descriptions |
| Per-project overrides | `.jcodemunch.jsonc` | Merges over global config, committed to repo |

### Schema changes

| Old (per-tool parameter) | New (config-level) | Tokens saved |
|--------------------------|-------------------|--------------|
| `suppress_meta: boolean` on every tool | `meta_fields` config key | **~594 tokens/turn** (removed from all 27 tool schemas) |

The legacy `suppress_meta` per-call parameter still works at runtime for backward compatibility — it is just no longer advertised in the schema.

### Config resolution order

```
1. Hardcoded defaults
2. Global config.jsonc       — overwrites defaults
3. Project .jcodemunch.jsonc — merges over global, per-repo
4. Environment variables     — FALLBACK only (fills gaps, doesn't override)
5. CLI flags                 — highest priority
```

Env vars act as fallback (not override) so a global `JCODEMUNCH_MAX_FOLDER_FILES=10000` in someone's shell profile cannot silently break a project's tuned `max_folder_files: 500`. Existing env vars emit a one-time deprecation warning.

### CLI

```bash
jcodemunch-mcp config          # print effective config with source tracking
jcodemunch-mcp config --init   # generate commented template
jcodemunch-mcp config --check  # validate config + prerequisites
```

## How

### New module: `config.py` (687 lines)

- JSONC parser: strips `//` and `/* */` comments, handles trailing commas, escaped quotes
- Type-validated loading with per-key warnings for invalid types
- Hash-based project config caching (avoids re-parsing on watcher reindex cycles)
- Thread-safe project config mutations via `threading.Lock`
- `_resolve_repo_key()` with positive + negative caching for O(1) repo lookups
- Env var fallback with `_explicit_keys` tracking (only fills absent keys)

### Server integration

- `list_tools()`: filters `disabled_tools`, narrows `search_symbols` language enum, applies description overrides, auto-disables `search_columns` when SQL not in languages
- `call_tool()`: project-level tool rejection via `is_tool_disabled(name, repo=repo_arg)`, config-driven `meta_fields` handling
- `_run_config()`: displays effective config grouped by concern with source tracking (default/config/env)

### Parser integration

- `parse_file()` in `extractor.py`: general `is_language_enabled()` gate — skips tree-sitter entirely for unconfigured languages (was previously only gating SQL)
- `security.py`: `get_max_folder_files()` reads from config, removed unsafe `max_index_files` fallback (could jump from 2K to 10K)

### Upstream merge (v1.10.18-19)

- Integrated `resolve_repo` tool and `JCODEMUNCH_PATH_MAP` into the config system
- `path_map` added to DEFAULTS, CONFIG_TYPES, ENV_VAR_MAPPING
- `parse_path_map()` reads from config first, env var as fallback

## Benchmarks

Profiled against a repo with 358 Python files and 5,847 symbols (7 iterations, median):

### Warm index (normal operation)

| Metric | Main | Config branch | Change |
|--------|------|--------------|--------|
| Total (16 tools) | 164.44 ms | 163.01 ms | **-0.9%** |

No measurable overhead from the config system. `config.get()` is a single dict lookup.

### Cold index (first load from disk)

| Metric | Main | Config branch | Change |
|--------|------|--------------|--------|
| Total (16 tools) | 1040.06 ms | 1039.64 ms | **-0.0%** |

### Language filtering (`languages: ["python"]`)

| Metric | All languages | Python only | Change |
|--------|--------------|-------------|--------|
| Symbols parsed | 11 | 4 | **-64%** |
| Index time | 76.2 ms | 58.1 ms | **-24%** |
| `search_columns` available | Yes | No (auto-gated) | |

### Bugs found and fixed during review

- `_resolve_repo_key()` cache miss: caused `IndexStore.list_repos()` scan on every tool call (50% regression, fixed)
- `_apply_description_overrides()` crashed on flat string format (`AttributeError`)
- Mixed f-string/%-format in logger calls
- `get_max_folder_files()` fallback could jump from 2,000 to 10,000

## Test plan

- [x] `pytest` — 1104 passed, 4 skipped
- [x] New test file: `test_config.py` (89 tests covering JSONC parser, loading, type validation, project merge, env var fallback, language/tool gating, template generation)
- [x] New server tests: description overrides (7 tests), disabled tools (2), meta fields (3), SQL gating (1), language enum (1)
- [x] Security tests: `get_max_folder_files` config integration (6 new tests)
- [x] Benchmark: warm/cold index regression profiling, language filter profiling
- [x] Manual: `config`, `config --init`, `config --check` CLI commands
- [x] Manual: flat + nested description override formats
- [x] Manual: `languages: ["python"]` gates SQL extractor, dbt provider, and search_columns

## Documentation

- `CONFIGURATION.md`: complete config guide (resolution order, all 25 keys, migration guide)
- `README.md`: updated with config file reference

---

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

pullRequest.title: fix: debug logging for skip reasons + exclude_secret_patterns config
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Closes #167

- **Add debug logging** for three silent skip paths in `discover_local_files`:
  - `skip_dir` — directory pruning via `SKIP_DIRS_REGEX` (was completely silent)
  - `skip_file` — file pattern skips via `SKIP_FILES_REGEX` (was completely silent)
  - `secret` — add `logger.debug()` alongside existing `warnings.append()`
- **Add `skip_dir` and `skip_file` counters** to `skip_counts` dict so they appear in the discovery summary
- **Add `exclude_secret_patterns` config option** — a list of `SECRET_PATTERNS` entries to suppress, giving users an immediate workaround for the `*secret*` full-path match false positive

## Motivation

A Go monorepo had 19 source files silently excluded because their parent directory contained "secret" in the name (e.g., `models/eventsource_security_otpauthsecret_v1/aggregate.go`). The `*secret*` glob in `SECRET_PATTERNS` matches the full relative path, not just the filename. With no debug logging on directory pruning or secret skips, this was very difficult to diagnose.

## Usage

Add to `~/.code-index/config.jsonc`:
```jsonc
{
  "exclude_secret_patterns": ["*secret*"]
}
```

Run with `JCODEMUNCH_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG` to see all skip decisions:
```
DEBUG:jcodemunch_mcp.tools.index_folder:SKIP skip_dir: grpchandlers/token/tokenadminhandler/testdata
DEBUG:jcodemunch_mcp.tools.index_folder:SKIP secret: cmd/eventsourcesecurityotpauthsecretv1/config_gen.go
DEBUG:jcodemunch_mcp.tools.index_folder:SKIP skip_file: ./go.sum
```

## Test plan

- [x] 41 existing secret-related tests pass
- [x] 26 existing discover/skip tests pass
- [x] Verified on real Go monorepo: 19 false-positive secret skips eliminated with config
- [x] Verified `.env`, `.pem`, `id_rsa` still caught when `*secret*` is excluded

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Windows/PowerShell users now have native hook equivalents — shared module (JcmHooks.psm1) plus read guard, edit guard, and index hook — alongside the existing bash scripts. Installation, execution policy, and verify steps are included. Logging & Debugging section updated with PS equivalents.

pullRequest.title: perf: retire unused staleness tools/fields; fix watcher config layering, hash-cache skip, test isolation, and config-driven watcher
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Remove client-facing staleness machinery (2 MCP tools + 3 _meta fields) that no client consumed, fix three watcher regressions introduced since 979f5b5, and make all watcher parameters configurable via `config.jsonc` so users can drop CLI flags from their MCP client configs.

## Changes

### 1. Retire staleness _meta injection and client-facing freshness tools

**Problem:** The previous design exposed reindex state to clients via:
- Three fields injected into every tool response `_meta`: `index_stale`, `reindex_in_progress`, `stale_since_ms`
- Two dedicated MCP tools: `wait_for_fresh` and `check_freshness`

No client ever acted on these fields — they were annotated noise on every single response.

**Token cost:** Two extra tools in the schema cost ~400 tokens per call just in definitions. Three staleness fields in `_meta` added ~30-50 tokens per response. In a typical 50-100 tool call session: 25,000-45,000 wasted tokens for information nobody was consuming.

**Better model:** Freshness management belongs on the server side. The config key `freshness_mode` already covers both cases:
- `"relaxed"` (default) — serve cached index immediately, zero overhead
- `"strict"` — block silently in `call_tool` via `await_freshness_if_strict()` until watcher finishes, then serve fresh results

Either way, the client receives a clean response. The server decides transparently.

**Removed:**
- `wait_for_fresh` MCP tool (schema + `call_tool` handler)
- `check_freshness` MCP tool + `tools/check_freshness.py`
- Entire `_meta` staleness injection block in `call_tool` (~50 lines)
- `_EXCLUDED_FROM_STRICT` entry for `wait_for_fresh`
- 670 lines of tests covering the removed surface area
- `get_reindex_status` / `wait_for_fresh_result` imports from `server.py`

**Preserved:**
- `reindex_state.py` machinery intact (watcher still needs it internally)
- `await_freshness_if_strict` in `call_tool` (strict mode still works)
- `freshness_mode` config key and `JCODEMUNCH_FRESHNESS_MODE` env var
- `meta_fields` config for partial `_meta` field selection

### 2. Fix watcher config layering

`_get_watcher_enabled()` bypassed `config_module.get()` and read `JCODEMUNCH_WATCH` env var directly, then fell back to hardcoded `False`. The config file `"watch"` key was silently ignored.

**Fix:** CLI flag > config (with env var as fallback only when key absent from `config.jsonc`).

### 3. Fix hash-cache reindex bug (silent skips)

When the watcher's in-memory hash cache missed, the fallback read the file from disk. But by the time `watchfiles` delivers the event the file already has new content, making `old_hash == new_hash` and silently skipping the change.

**Fix:** Use a `"__cache_miss__"` sentinel that keeps the fast path active (<200ms) while guaranteeing the file is re-parsed rather than skipped.

### 4. Fix flaky tests from SQLite WAL cache contamination

Tests that modify the DB directly (`index_version`, `indexed_at`, corruption) didn't invalidate the in-memory LRU cache. On Windows, WAL mode doesn't always update the DB file mtime on write, so the cache key still matched.

**Fix:** Added `tests/conftest.py` with autouse fixtures for cache clear and config reset, plus targeted `_cache_evict()` calls after direct DB modifications. Also fixed `test_openai_summarizer_timeout_config` that was reading `config.get()` for `allow_remote_summarizer` but the env var never flowed through `load_config()`.

### 5. Config-driven watcher parameters

**Problem:** Even though `"watch": true` in `config.jsonc` enables the embedded watcher, all other watcher parameters (`debounce`, `paths`, `idle_timeout`, `extra_ignore`, `follow_symlinks`, `log`) were only sourced from CLI flags. This forced verbose flags in MCP client configs:

```jsonc
// Before: every param must be a CLI flag
"command": ["uvx", "--with", "jcodemunch-mcp[watch]", "jcodemunch-mcp", "serve",
            "--watcher", "--watcher-no-ai-summaries"]
```

**Fix:** The `watcher_kwargs` block in `main()` reads each parameter as: **CLI flag > config key > default**. Same layering pattern already used for `watch` and `freshness_mode`.

New config keys (alphabetical after `watch`):
- `watch_debounce_ms` (default: 2000) — existed in config.py but was not wired to watcher_kwargs
- `watch_extra_ignore` — list of gitignore-style patterns (default: `[]`)
- `watch_follow_symlinks` — bool (default: `false`)
- `watch_idle_timeout` — minutes, `null` = disabled
- `watch_log` — file path or `"auto"` (default: `null`)
- `watch_paths` — list of folder paths (default: `["."]` i.e. CWD)

CLI flags (`--watcher-*`) remain as overrides for all keys.

**Result:** Users configure the watcher entirely in `config.jsonc`:

```jsonc
// config.jsonc
{
  "watch": true,
  "use_ai_summaries": false,
  "watch_debounce_ms": 2000
}
```

```jsonc
// MCP client config — simplified
"command": ["uvx", "--with", "jcodemunch-mcp[watch]", "jcodemunch-mcp"]
```

Note: `jcodemunch-mcp[watch]` extra is still required to install `watchfiles`. The server exits with a clear error if `watch: true` is set but `watchfiles` is not installed.

## Impact

| Metric | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Schema tokens per call | +~400 (2 unused tools) | 0 |
| _meta tokens per response | +~30-50 (3 staleness fields) | 0 |
| Lines of code | +880 | -880 (net deletion) |
| Tests passing | 1256 | 1281, 0 regressions |
| Watcher config layering | Broken (config file ignored) | Fixed |
| Hash-cache miss handling | Silent skip | Re-parse guaranteed |
| Test isolation (Windows) | Flaky from cache bleed | Clean via autouse fixtures |
| Watcher config | CLI flags only | config.jsonc with CLI override |

## Files changed

```
# Deleted
src/jcodemunch_mcp/tools/check_freshness.py  deleted (108 lines)
tests/test_backpressure_regression.py        deleted (361 lines)
tests/test_check_freshness.py                deleted (144 lines)
tests/test_wait_for_fresh.py                 deleted (149 lines)

# Modified / added
AGENT_HOOKS.md                               doc cleanup (−2 lines)
CONFIGURATION.md                             new watcher config key docs (+11/−1)
README.md                                    doc cleanup (+2/−1)
SPEC.md                                      remove staleness/freshness tool specs (−92 lines)
USER_GUIDE.md                                doc cleanup (−5 lines)
src/jcodemunch_mcp/config.py                 new watcher config keys (+20)
src/jcodemunch_mcp/server.py                 staleness removal + config-driven watcher kwargs (−97/+39)
src/jcodemunch_mcp/watcher.py                cache miss sentinel (+19)
tests/conftest.py                            autouse cache/config fixtures (+48, new)
tests/test_config_watcher_keys.py            config key defaults/types (+36, new)
tests/test_find_importers.py                 isolation fix (+8)
tests/test_hardening.py                      isolation fix (+4)
tests/test_retrieval_tools.py                isolation fix (+4)
tests/test_server.py                         tool removal assertions (+19)
tests/test_storage.py                        isolation fix (+3)
tests/test_summarizer.py                     config flow fix (+10)
tests/test_watcher_memory_cache.py           cache miss sentinel tests (+64, new)
tests/test_watcher_serve.py                  config layering + CLI override tests (+304/−45)
```

Fixes #177

pullRequest.title: feat: session-aware routing — negative evidence, session journal, plan_turn, turn budget, session persistence
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Closes #205  (session-aware routing issue).

Adds a session-aware routing layer on top of jCodeMunch's existing BM25 + PageRank retrieval. Previously the AI received raw search results with no guidance — empty results were ambiguous, there was no session memory, and no token budget control. This PR gives the AI explicit signals, routing intelligence, and behavioral guardrails.

**Branch:** `feature/session-aware-routing`
**Stats:** 21 files changed, +2842 lines, 2161 tests pass (75 new, 0 regressions)

---

## The Core Problem

jCodeMunch answered "what exists?" but gave no guidance when features were **missing**. This caused a predictable failure chain:

```
Empty search → AI doesn't know if feature is missing or query is wrong
            → AI finds a vaguely related file and assumes it covers the feature
            → AI writes incorrect code in the wrong location
```

This PR breaks that chain at every step:

```
Empty search → negative_evidence says "verdict: no_implementation_found"
            → CLAUDE.md policy says "do NOT search again"
            → plan_turn says "add it in auth.py (centrality 0.85, 12 importers)"
            → AI writes correct code in the architecturally appropriate location
```

---

## Before / After (Detailed)

| Behavior | Before | After |
|----------|--------|-------|
| **AI searches for missing feature** | Empty result list, no explanation. AI re-searches with different terms or hallucinates that a related file covers it. | Structured `negative_evidence` with `verdict: "no_implementation_found"`, `scanned_symbols` count, and `related_existing` files. AI knows definitively the feature doesn't exist. |
| **AI explores the same files repeatedly** | No memory. Every turn starts cold. Same files re-read, same queries re-run. | Session journal tracks all reads/searches/edits. `get_session_context` shows what was already explored. `plan_turn` reports `session_overlap`. |
| **AI makes 15+ tool calls burning tokens** | No feedback on cumulative token usage. Each call independent. | Turn budget accumulator warns at 80% (`budget_warning` in `_meta`), flags `auto_compacted` when exhausted. |
| **AI doesn't know where to start** | Calls `search_symbols` or `get_ranked_context` blindly. | Calls `plan_turn` first — gets confidence level, recommended symbols/files, and exploration budget in one call. |
| **AI doesn't know WHERE to add missing code** | No guidance. Picks a file by gut feeling. | `plan_turn` returns `insertion_candidates` — top files ranked by PageRank centrality and importer count. |
| **AI re-searches a query that already returned nothing** | No memory of previous zero-result searches. Same dead end explored again. | `plan_turn` detects prior evidence: `confidence: "none"`, `"This exact query was already searched 3 times with 0 results. Do NOT search again."` |
| **Turn budget running low** | No awareness. AI keeps making full-size requests. | `plan_turn` returns `budget_advisor`: top 5 unexplored files ranked by PageRank. `"Budget 82% used. Focus remaining reads on these."` |
| **AI edits files, index goes stale** | File watcher handles reindexing (200ms), but requires a separate process. PostToolUse hook provides zero-delay reindexing without needing the watcher. Both can coexist safely — index-file is a no-op on fresh files.. | PostToolUse hook auto-runs `jcodemunch-mcp index-file` on Edit/Write/NotebookEdit. `register_edit` available for bulk cache invalidation. |
| **Server restarts lose all session context** | Cold start every time. | `session_resume: true` persists journal + search cache + negative evidence log. Validated against `indexed_at` on restore. |
| **Repeated search for same query** | BM25 recomputed from scratch every time. | LRU result cache (configurable, default 128 entries). Cache hit returns in <1ms. |

---

## Expected Impact (Estimated)

| Area | Estimated Improvement | Reasoning |
|------|:---------------------:|-----------|
| **Token efficiency** | **-30-50%** per session | Turn budget caps waste; result cache eliminates redundant BM25; session journal prevents re-reading same files |
| **Redundant dead-end searches** | **-90%+** | Prior negative evidence detection (`confidence: "none"`) blocks repeat searches for queries that already returned 0 results |
| **AI accuracy on "create new feature" tasks** | **Significant** | The hallucination chain (empty result → wrong assumption → wrong code) is broken by explicit negative evidence + CLAUDE.md policy enforcement |
| **First-call routing quality** | **New capability** | `plan_turn` with PageRank-based insertion points didn't exist before — AI had no "opening move" tool |
| **Index freshness during editing** | **Automatic** | Was manual (AI had to remember to call `index_file`); now automatic via PostToolUse hook |

These are estimates based on the failure modes addressed. Actual measurement requires running the same tasks before and after and comparing outcomes.

---

## Unique Architectural Advantages

These capabilities are enabled by jCodeMunch's import graph + PageRank analysis — capabilities that pure text-similarity retrieval systems cannot provide:

1. **Insertion point recommendation**: When `plan_turn` returns `confidence: "low"`, it uses PageRank to suggest WHERE to add the missing feature, ranked by architectural centrality. "No CSRF found. Suggested location: `src/middleware/auth.py` (centrality 0.85, highest in middleware/)."

2. **Smart budget advisor**: When turn budget exceeds 60%, `plan_turn` returns the highest-value unexplored files ranked by PageRank — the AI knows exactly which remaining reads will give the most architectural insight.

3. **Prior negative evidence**: `confidence: "none"` with provable "this query already returned 0 results N times" — prevents infinite re-search loops. The evidence persists across turns and (with `session_resume`) across restarts.

---

## What Changed

### New Tools (3)

| Tool | What it does |
|------|-------------|
| `plan_turn` | Opening-move router. Takes a query, runs BM25 + PageRank, returns: confidence level (high/medium/low/none), recommended symbols + files, insertion point suggestions for missing features, smart budget advisor (unexplored high-value files), prior negative evidence detection. |
| `register_edit` | Post-edit cache invalidation. Clears BM25 token cache, result cache, and import index for edited files. Records edits in session journal. |
| `get_session_context` | Returns session history: files read, searches run, edits made, tool call counts. |

### New Modules (3)

| Module | What it does |
|--------|-------------|
| `session_journal.py` | Process-lifetime singleton. Tracks reads, searches, edits, tool calls, negative evidence. Bounded at 5000 entries per category with LRU eviction. Thread-safe. |
| `turn_budget.py` | Cross-call token accumulator. Detects turn boundaries via configurable gap. Injects `budget_warning` + `auto_compacted` into `_meta` when budget runs low. Exposes `percent_used()`, `should_compact()`, `configure()` public API. |
| `session_state.py` | Save/restore session across restarts. Writes only on clean shutdown via `atexit` (NVME-friendly — no periodic flush). Staleness validated via `indexed_at` (works for both git and non-git projects). |

### Enhancements to Existing Code

**`search_symbols.py`:**
- LRU result cache (`_result_cache`, 128 entries default, configurable). Cache key includes `indexed_at` for automatic invalidation on reindex. Returns shallow copies to prevent mutation.
- Negative evidence: when results are empty or below threshold, response includes `negative_evidence` dict with `verdict` ("no_implementation_found" / "low_confidence_matches"), `scanned_symbols`, `scanned_files`, `related_existing`.
- All thresholds config-driven (`negative_evidence_threshold`, `search_result_cache_max`).

**`server.py`:**
- Turn budget recording after every tool call — injects `budget_warning`, `turn_tokens_used`, `turn_budget_remaining`, `auto_compacted` into `_meta`.
- Journal recording: file reads (including batch `symbol_ids`), searches, negative evidence collection.
- Session state lifecycle: `_restore_session_state()` on startup, `_save_session_state()` on shutdown via `atexit`.
- Uses `list_repos()` metadata for index staleness checks (no full index load at startup).

**`config.py`:**
- 10 new keys in `DEFAULTS`, `CONFIG_TYPES`, and `generate_template()`.
- New tools added to `all_tools` list for `disabled_tools` template.
- New "Session-Aware Routing" section in config template.

**`cli/init.py`:**
- CLAUDE.md policy: routing rules (call `plan_turn` first, obey confidence, handle negative evidence, watch for `budget_warning`).
- PostToolUse hook: `Edit|Write|NotebookEdit` triggers `jcodemunch-mcp index-file $CLAUDE_FILE_PATH`.
- `install_hooks()` merges both `_WORKTREE_HOOKS` and `_TOOL_HOOKS`.

### Prior Art: AGENT_HOOKS.md

The PostToolUse hook for auto-reindexing builds on the pattern documented in `AGENT_HOOKS.md` (PR #156) and the `index-file` CLI subcommand (commit 95a8971). This PR automates the installation via `jcodemunch-mcp init --hooks` using Claude Code's native `settings.json` hook format, making it cross-platform and zero-copy. The `PreToolUse` guards from `AGENT_HOOKS.md` (read guard, edit guard) remain complementary — they are not duplicated here.

**Note:** The PostToolUse hook is Claude Code-specific. Other agent harnesses (Codex CLI, OpenCode, Cursor) should use `register_edit` for cache invalidation after edits.

### Config Keys (10)

| Key | Default | Purpose |
|-----|---------|---------|
| `negative_evidence_threshold` | `0.5` | BM25 score below which negative evidence is emitted |
| `search_result_cache_max` | `128` | LRU cache size (0 = disable) |
| `session_journal` | `true` | Enable/disable session tracking |
| `plan_turn_high_threshold` | `2.0` | BM25 score for "high" confidence |
| `plan_turn_medium_threshold` | `0.5` | BM25 score for "medium" confidence |
| `turn_budget_tokens` | `20000` | Max tokens per turn (0 = disable) |
| `turn_gap_seconds` | `30.0` | Silence before new turn starts |
| `session_resume` | `false` | Persist session across restarts |
| `session_max_age_minutes` | `30` | Discard saved state older than this |
| `session_max_queries` | `50` | Cap on persisted cache entries |

---

## Test Plan

- [x] `python -m pytest tests/ -x -q` — 2161 passed, 5 skipped, 0 failures
- [x] 75 new tests across 10 test files
- [x] Thread safety: concurrent write tests on SessionJournal, TurnBudget, result cache
- [x] Session state roundtrip: save, restart, restore, verify counts match
- [x] Negative evidence: zero-result search returns structured verdict
- [x] plan_turn: high/medium/low/none confidence for matching/missing/repeated queries
- [x] Result cache: hit returns same data, invalidated by register_edit
- [x] PostToolUse hook: correct matcher pattern and command
- [x] Config: all 10 keys in DEFAULTS, CONFIG_TYPES, generate_template
- [ ] Manual smoke test: start MCP server, run plan_turn with known and unknown queries
- [ ] Manual: verify budget_warning appears after many tool calls in one turn

Co-Authored: Claude Opus 4.6 (nerfed but still functional) as planner / reviewer
Co-Authored: GLM 5 (executor)

pullRequest.title: feat(security): add exclude_skip_directories config to override built-in skip list
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- Add `exclude_skip_directories` config key that removes entries from the built-in skip directory list at call time
- Mirrors the existing `exclude_secret_patterns` pattern
- Fixes the `proto/` directory being unconditionally skipped, blocking protobuf projects

Fixes #209

## Changes

- **`security.py`**: `get_skip_directories()`, `get_skip_patterns()`, and `_excluded_skip_directories()` — filter lists by reading config at call time
- **`index_folder.py`**: replaced module-level `SKIP_DIRS_REGEX` with `_build_skip_dirs_regex()` called per-index
- **`index_repo.py`**: `should_skip_file()` calls `get_skip_patterns()` instead of static `SKIP_PATTERNS`
- **`config.py`**: added key to defaults, type validation, and config template

## Test plan

- [x] 6 new tests in `TestExcludeSkipDirectories` covering default behavior, single/multi exclusion, pattern filtering, and empty config
- [x] 1 new test in `test_tools.py` verifying `should_skip_file` respects the config for the GitHub indexer path
- [x] Existing `test_should_skip_file` still passes (no regression)

pullRequest.title: feat: hook-event creates git worktrees + worktree_base_path config
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Closes #216

- `hook-event create` now runs `git worktree add` with a `worktree-{name}` branch and prints the resolved path to stdout (required by Claude Code)
- `hook-event remove` runs `git worktree remove --force` then `git branch -D worktree-{name}` to clean up
- New config key `worktree_base_path` in config.jsonc overrides where worktrees are created (default: `{cwd}/.claude/worktrees/{name}`)
- Manifest moved from `~/.claude/` to `~/.code-index/` (respects `CODE_INDEX_PATH`); legacy manifest auto-migrated
- All paths use `pathlib.Path` for Windows compatibility; no shell=True or bash-isms

## Test plan

- [x] `pytest tests/test_watch_claude.py -v` -- 30 passed
- [x] Manual: `echo '{"cwd":"/path/to/repo","name":"test-wt","hook_event_name":"WorktreeCreate"}' | jcodemunch-mcp hook-event create` -- creates worktree, prints path
- [x] Manual: `claude -w` creates worktree and launches session successfully
- [ ] Set `worktree_base_path` in config.jsonc, verify worktree created there
- [ ] `jcodemunch-mcp config --upgrade` adds `worktree_base_path` to existing config

pullRequest.title: feat: audit_agent_config discovers .claude/rules/ directory
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- Adds `.claude/rules/*.md` (project) and `~/.claude/rules/*.md` (global) to `audit_agent_config` file discovery
- All existing checks (token cost, stale symbols, dead paths, bloat, scope leaks, redundancy) now run against rule files
- Includes test for project rules directory discovery

## Context

Claude Code loads every `.md` file in `.claude/rules/` into every agent turn. In my setup, 17 rule files contain ~10,800 tokens — 60% more than the 3 files the audit currently scans. Stale file path references in rule files went undetected because they weren't in scope.

Closes #234

## Test plan

- [x] All 35 existing `test_audit_agent_config.py` tests pass
- [x] New `test_discover_files_project_rules_dir` verifies `.md` files are found, non-`.md` files are ignored
- [x] Verified on Windows 11 with 17 project rule files

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
